title: Acme Radiology Ops
verdict: Strong early signal in teleradiology groups migrating off spreadsheet-based worklist triage; timing is good due to a recent staffing crunch pattern showing up across three independent sources.
primary_icp: Operations lead at a mid-size teleradiology group (10-50 radiologists) managing worklist prioritization manually.
adjacent_icp: Solo/small imaging center admin evaluating AI triage tools for the first time.
disqualifiers: Groups already under contract with a competing triage vendor; academic centers requiring multi-year procurement cycles.

top_prospect.name: J. Rivera

evidence.text: Public forum post describing manual spreadsheet triage breaking down under case volume
Job posting for 'Radiology Workflow Coordinator' suggesting scaling pain

top_prospect.opener: Hi J., saw your post about the spreadsheet triage process breaking down as case volume grew -- we built a worklist tool for exactly that problem. Would a 10-minute look be useful?

patterns: Multiple groups mention spreadsheet-based triage failing above ~200 cases/day
outreach_plan: Day 1: Send opener to top 3 prospects
Day 7: Review response rate and refine ICP
limitations: Sample limited to public forum and job-board sources; no direct outreach to verify budget authority.
